_provenance.note: RECORDED LIVE Gemini run, one merchant per blocker (real model output). FICTIONAL demo names (the public demo never attaches synthetic high-risk/stalled states to real businesses); the real-data capability is in lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts. Frozen because a live call is not recomputable. The LIVE_AI vs FAILED_TO_FALLBACK split + gate verdicts are non-deterministic across a refresh.
honest_findings: Claims-gatekeeper passed every DECLARED claim against merchant data on every produced draft (its core function held on live output).

AUTHENTIC failure handling on real output: schema-constrained generation occasionally returns unparseable output -> FAILED_TO_FALLBACK to the deterministic stub, with billed cost still recorded so the cumulative cap stays honest. High-risk drafts are held for human approval (WARN).

Live runs surfaced a guardrail OVER-match: the pinned core flagged phrases like "business verification", "menu to be uploaded", "photos to complete" regardless of tense, false-blocking truthful drafts. FIXED in the agent tier (lib/agents/state-consistency.ts — a completion word must follow the step noun via a done-auxiliary), pinned core/differential UNTOUCHED. Confirmed here: 0 false blocks.

A no-leakage / register grader (added after this recording; lib/evals/draft-quality.ts) re-scores the recorded MERCHANT-FACING prose and CATCHES a real register leak in 3 of the 6 drafts: Mission Masa surfaced the raw "bank_verification_needed" enum + a "Medium risk" disclosure; Fog City Tacos and Bayview Bistro disclosed an internal risk level. Those now score 3/4, the clean drafts 4/4 — an authentic eval catch on real model output, reproducible from the frozen bodies (evals/live-samples.test.ts). The live prompt was also tightened to forbid such leaks going forward.

rows.subject: Action Required: Verify Your Business for Curbside Commons
rows.body: Hello Fog City Tacos,Your account is currently flagged as High Risk. To proceed with Curbside Commons, your business information needs verification.You have completed 0 of 5 steps in the onboarding process. Please complete the business verification step to continue.Thank you,The Curbside Commons Team

rows.subject: Next step for Bayview Bistro: Upload your menu
rows.body: Hello Bayview Bistro,Welcome to Curbside Commons!You've completed 1 of your 5 setup steps. Your Restaurant is currently blocked from going live because the menu needs to be uploaded. We've noted this as a high-risk item for account completion.The next best action for you is to upload your menu.The Curbside Commons Team

rows.subject: Action Needed: Add Photos to Your Curbside Commons Profile
rows.body: Hello Marina Morsels,To help complete your setup as a Restaurant on Curbside Commons, please add photos to your profile. This is currently the next step to progress.You have completed 2 out of 5 steps in the onboarding process.Thanks,The Curbside Commons Team

rows.subject: Important: Set your hours, Sunset Noodle House
rows.body: Hi Sunset Noodle House,To get your Restaurant listed on Curbside Commons, please set your business hours. This is the next step in your onboarding process. You have completed 3 out of 5 steps.

rows.subject: Action needed for your Curbside Commons account
rows.body: Hi Mission Masa,This is a quick update regarding your Curbside Commons account.To fully activate your account and process transactions, your bank account needs verification. This is a crucial step for a Restaurant like yours.You have completed 4 out of 5 onboarding steps. The current blocker is bank_verification_needed. Completing this will help mitigate the Medium risk associated with unverified information.Your next best action is to verify your bank.Thank you,The Curbside Commons Team

rows.errorClass: No object generated: could not parse the response.
rows.subject: Your next onboarding step with Curbside Commons
rows.body: Hi Embarcadero Eats, thanks for getting started on your Curbside Commons onboarding. Your next step is to complete your final verification. Reply to this message if you would like a hand.
